desc: Munnar is Kerala's highland tea-country escape, prized for cooler temperatures, plantation scenery, and wildlife access. It sees dual seasonality: winter and holiday peaks for clear viewpoints, and greener but rainier monsoon months with lower crowd pressure.
hls: Lush monsoon scenery with lower prices
Great base for 2–3 day nature breaks

todo.name: Tea museum + estate visit
todo.desc: Learn tea history and walk viewpoints through active plantations.

todo.name: Eravikulam National Park
todo.desc: Book timed entry for Nilgiri tahr sightings and rolling highland views.

todo.name: Top Station sunrise
todo.desc: Start early for valley cloudscapes before day-trip traffic builds.

todo.name: Mattupetty–Echo Point circuit
todo.desc: Pair reservoir viewpoints with short forest-edge walks.

todo.name: Tea-town café afternoon
todo.desc: Use cool evenings for relaxed café and bakery hopping.

todo.name: Anamudi foothill viewpoints
todo.desc: Choose short highland trails in drier months for clearer visibility.

wifi.note: Town hotels are usually workable, but estate stays and heavy-rain periods can reduce speed and stability.

prac.fltNote: Typical UK routings connect into Kochi (COK) before a 4–5+ hour mountain-road transfer. Return fares are often ~£420–£690 off-peak, ~£690–£940 in shoulder demand, and ~£940+ around December–January holiday peaks.
prac.lang: Malayalam, Tamil, English common in tourism
prac.visa: India eVisa is available for many nationalities; verify current rules before departure.
alerts: Monsoon rain and fog can affect road safety and reduce visibility at key viewpoints.
Holiday weekends can produce heavy traffic on approach roads from Kochi.
National-park slots and popular viewpoint entries may require advance booking in peak months.

sweet: December to March is best for clearer views and cooler weather; monsoon months suit green-season stays if you accept rain disruption risk.
